25.19 × 3 + 4.41 − 0.9 = ?

drop: Requires the parallelogram diagram showing diagonal segment expressions; cannot be reliably linearised to text.

q: In which state is the 'Chalo Loku' festival celebrated?

100 × 121 + 11 − 7 ÷ 24 − 8 × 33 × 99 ÷ 17 = ?

Method — Mode of action
A. Copper-T — i) Egg will not reach uterus
B. Oral pills — ii) Sperms do not reach the cervix
C. Condom — iii) Prevent implantation
D. Tubectomy — iv) Prevent fertilization
The Periyar River flows in which of the following Indian states?

In economics, the slope of the demand curve is typically ________.
o: Curved moving from left to right
Straight parallel to X axis from left to right

Rajan started from a point and walked a distance of 200 m towards the north, he then turned left and walked 300 m, turned right and walked 400 m, and then turned left and walked 1 km. In which direction is Rajan facing now? (All turns are 90 degree turns only)

drop: Requires reading values off the bar chart of yearly production; cannot be linearised to text.

In which of the following reactions of respiration is oxygen required?
o: Conversion of pyruvate to CO₂, H₂O and energy

q: P and Q alone can complete a piece of work in 9 days and 12 days, respectively. In how many days will the work be completed if they work on alternate days starting with Q?

E, F, G, H, I and X are sitting in a straight row, facing north. H is second to the right of G. F and X are immediate neighbours. Both F and X are immediate neighbours of I. H and X are immediate neighbours. X is at the extreme right end of the row. I is at the immediate left of H. Who is at the immediate left of I?

Six persons A, B, C, D, E and F, are sitting around a circular table. Only two persons are sitting between B and A. F is to the immediate right of C. E is second to the right of C. E is second to the left of A. Who is sitting to the immediate right of E?

A statement is given followed by two assumptions numbered I and II. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, and decide which of the given assumptions is/are implicit in the statement.
Statement:
Please replace the rent letter by 1) April to collect free passes. Passes will not be issued without valid address proof.
Assumption:
I. Voter ID card is a valid address proof.
II. Passes can be purchased at the venue.
o: Both assumptions I and II are implicit
Neither assumption I nor II is implicit

q: The difference in molecular mass between two consecutive homologous series members will be.

o: The latitude lines are parallel to each other.
180° E and 180° W refer to the same longitudinal line.

q: The Indian Constitution gives the President the authority to declare three types of emergencies. Which of following is NOT among them?

Three statements are followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. You have to consider these statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, and decide which of the given conclusions logically follow/s from the given statements.
Statements:
All pens are pens.
All pens are brushes.
All brushes are tables.
Conclusions:
I) Some tables are pens.
II) All pens are pens.
III) No pen is a pen.
o: Either conclusion I or conclusion III follows

U visited Uttar Pradesh exactly two months prior to P. Y preferred the month of July. J preferred to visit Rajasthan exactly between the months preferred by Y and U. L visited exactly one month prior to Y. In which month did K visit?
